nodes.name: generate_repo_hierarchy_diagram.py
nodes.summary: Generates the repository hierarchy diagram (SVG + draw.io) used in report/docs.

nodes.name: generate_sensitivity_jobs.py
nodes.summary: Generates decoding-sensitivity sbatch jobs (temperature/multi-seed sweep) plus the matching judge-scoring sbatch for TC1.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Emits per-variant sbatch files honoring the TC1 bootstrap block and offline env.

nodes.name: genlen_512_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Generation-length study comparing the 128- vs 512-token budgets: per-pair kappa, McNemar, BH-FDR, and the prefix-truncation analysis that established the 128-era Qwen +0.055 ASR increase as a truncation artefact (report section 6.16).

nodes.name: prefix_truncation_block
nodes.summary: Tests whether 128-token verdict flips are explained by judging truncated prefixes of the 512 outputs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Runs all comparison blocks (ASR, capability, length, kappa, truncation) and prints the study summary.

nodes.name: harmbench_category_breakdown.py
nodes.summary: Per-category HarmBench ASR breakdown (judge-scored) across pairs, showing which harm categories drive deltas.

nodes.name: harness_eval.py
nodes.summary: Named wrapper (make harness-eval) running the focused harness self-evaluation test suite.

nodes.name: human_label_audit.py
nodes.summary: Human annotation audit tooling (T30): draws a stratified candidate sample into a labeling sheet (CSV/HTML), then scores human labels against the judge and v2 regex with kappa and precision/recall/F1, and applies adjudicated labels.

nodes.name: make_sheet
nodes.summary: Builds the stratified human-labeling sheet from judge/regex disagreements and agreements.

nodes.name: score
nodes.summary: Scores collected human labels against judge and regex outcomes (kappa, PRF).

nodes.name: apply_labels
nodes.summary: Applies adjudicated human labels back into the audit artifacts.

nodes.name: make_figures.py
nodes.summary: Matplotlib figure generation for the report: capability anchor, ASR forest plot, precision sweep, judge-vs-proxy comparison, category ASR, and multi-seed sensitivity figures.

nodes.name: mmlu_subject_breakdown.py
nodes.summary: Per-subject MMLU accuracy breakdown across pairs to localize capability changes under quantization.

nodes.name: number_bible_512.py
nodes.summary: Prints the authoritative 'number bible' of 512-primary values from results_512/analysis so every number inserted into the report/thesis can be cross-checked against one source.

nodes.name: prefetch_tc1.py
nodes.summary: Head-node pre-cache script: downloads datasets, model weights, and optionally the 13B judge into the HF cache so offline compute nodes (HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1) can run.

nodes.name: _collect_dataset_targets
nodes.summary: Collects dataset repos/configs referenced by the config's benchmarks.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Prefetches datasets and models per config; --judge adds the HarmBench classifier.

nodes.name: refusal_margin_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Analyzes captured refusal-margin sidecars: per-pair paired margin shifts, AUC of margin vs judge outcome, bootstrap CIs — the mechanistic complement to behavioural ASR deltas.

nodes.name: analyse_pair
nodes.summary: Computes paired margin-shift statistics and AUC for one model pair.

nodes.name: sensitivity_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Decoding-sensitivity analysis: compares greedy vs sampled-decoding deltas (v2 and judge ASR) to check robustness of the headline conclusions.

nodes.name: verify_report_claims.py
nodes.summary: MACHINE CLAIM LOCK (make verify-claims): ~55 checks pinning report and thesis numbers plus IEEE citation consistency to the primary artifacts in results_512/analysis; rides pytest via tests/test_report_claims.py so stale claims fail CI.

nodes.name: run_checks
nodes.summary: Runs every claim check (numbers, labels, citations) against live artifacts and reports mismatches.

nodes.name: Checker
nodes.summary: Accumulates check results and renders the pass/fail report.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the test suite.

nodes.name: test_int8_scoping.py
nodes.summary: Guards INT8 scoping: judge agreement, pairwise, and breakdown scripts must not reopen committed old-6 judge sidecars when scoring INT8 members (zero-drift rule).

nodes.name: test_judge_agreement.py
nodes.summary: Tests kappa computation in judge_agreement: known values, perfect agreement, total disagreement, degenerate and empty cases.

nodes.name: test_report_claims.py
nodes.summary: Pytest bridge that runs the machine claim lock (verify_report_claims) so report/thesis number drift fails the test suite.
